This unprecedented funding is intended to ensure the U.S. remains the world’s premier military power while advancing missile defense, drone technology, and space operations. The FY27 budget also highlights investments in the Gol

item.title: James Talarico Works to Secure Black Voter Support After Texas Democratic Primary Victory
item.description: Texas state Representative James Talarico, after narrowly defeating U.S. Representative Jasmine Crockett in the Democratic primary for the U.S. Senate seat, now faces the critical challenge of winning over Black voters who overwhelmingly supported his opponent. Crockett garnered strong enthusiasm among Black communities, capturing 57% of the vote in counties where at least one in five voting-age residents are Black, compared to Talarico's 42%, according to Reuters analysis. Democrats' prospects of flipping the Senate seat in deeply red Texas hinge on mobilizing the state's nearly 3 million Black voters for the general election. Talarico, a white seminarian and former teacher, is actively engaging these voters through grassroots efforts, backed by groups like Beto O'Rourke's Powered By People and Black state lawmakers. Supporters like Crystal Chism of the Texas Coalition of Black Democrats urge unity, stating there's no time to dwell on primary divisions and that electing Talarico is pa

item.title: Trump comments on security, political violence after shooting at White House correspondents’ dinner
item.description: President Donald Trump addressed reporters at the White House the day after a shooting incident at the White House correspondents’ dinner in Washington, describing the world as chaotic and reflecting on the dangers of public life. The incident occurred at the Washington Hilton, where Trump was set to speak at the annual media event; a gunman approached a security checkpoint outside the ballroom, fired shots, and was quickly subdued by Secret Service and law enforcement. A Secret Service agent wearing a bulletproof vest was struck but later released from medical care, and the suspected attacker, identified as 31‑year‑old Cole Allen from California, was taken into custody and faces multiple federal charges, including assaulting a federal officer and discharging a firearm. Trump said he initially mistook the sound of gunfire for a tray falling and called the episode “totally shocking,” while also suggesting that the suspect may have targeted him, citing his prior assassination‑attempt inj

item.title: Reports Suggest Kash Patel May Face Removal Amid Controversies and Internal Concerns
item.description:
According to a report citing a senior White House official, FBI Director Kash Patel may soon be removed from his position with President Trump’s approval, following a series of negative media reports and internal concerns about his conduct. The unnamed official indicated that the accumulation of unfavorable press coverage has frustrated the president and made Patel’s position increasingly untenable, suggesting his departure could be imminent.
The article outlines multiple controversies surrounding Patel, including allegations published by The Atlantic that describe excessive drinking, erratic behavior, and unexplained absences from duty. These claims, attributed to numerous current and former officials, raised concerns about his reliability and potential risks to public safety. Patel has strongly denied all allegations and filed a $250 million defamation lawsuit against the magazine, calling the report fabricated and malicious.
Additional incidents have contributed to scrutiny of Pat

item.title: K-9 units locate and rescue missing woman after 200-foot fall in San Bernardino Mountains
item.description:
Two trained K-9 units from the San Bernardino County Sheriff’s Department played a critical role in rescuing a missing 49-year-old woman who had fallen approximately 200 feet into a steep ravine in the San Bernardino Mountains. The incident began on the evening of April 18, when deputies were called to a large 500-acre property in Twin Peaks after the woman’s family reported her missing. She had reportedly left for a walk but did not return, and her relatives were unable to identify her last known location. The woman was also said to be suffering from a traumatic brain injury, which heightened concerns for her safety.
Deputies initially searched the expansive property for several hours without success. Recognizing the difficulty of locating someone in such rugged terrain, they requested assistance from two bloodhounds, Keva and Naji. Upon arrival, the dogs quickly got to work. Using a pillow to pick up the woman’s scent, K-9 Naji identified a starting point, and K-9 Keva then led depu

item.title: Jurupa Valley man dies after medical emergency in Riverside County jail
item.description:
A 38-year-old Jurupa Valley resident, identified as Brian Waterman, died after experiencing a medical emergency while in custody at the Robert Presley Detention Center in Riverside County, according to the Riverside County Sheriff’s Office. Waterman had been arrested Thursday evening by the Jurupa Valley Police Department, though authorities have not disclosed the reason for his arrest.
Officials said that on Friday, while being held in a jail cell, Waterman began suffering a medical emergency. Custody staff and jail medical personnel responded and provided aid at the scene. Despite these efforts, his condition worsened over the following day. On Saturday, April 25, he was transported to a hospital, where he was later pronounced dead.
The sheriff’s office has not provided details about the nature of the medical emergency or any underlying health conditions that may have contributed to his death. Authorities also stated that there were no immediate signs of foul play. Investigations a

item.title: President Trump discusses his reaction to the White House Correspondents' Dinner security incident
item.description:
In an interview with CBS News' '60 Minutes,' President Donald Trump reflected on the security incident that occurred at the White House Correspondents' Dinner, where he was swiftly evacuated after shots were fired at a security checkpoint. During the conversation, the President acknowledged that his reaction during the initial moments of the threat may have inadvertently complicated the Secret Service's evacuation process. He explained that his instinct was to assess the situation rather than immediately retreat, admitting he told agents to 'wait a minute' as he sought to understand what was occurring in the ballroom.
The incident involved a 31-year-old suspect named Cole Allen, who was apprehended by law enforcement after attempting to breach a checkpoint at the Washington Hilton hotel. While the President was successfully escorted out of the building by his security detail, he noted that he 'fought like hell' to continue the event, emphasizing his desire not to let the incident dis

item.title: Unusual Disruption in Panama’s Ocean Upwelling Raises Climate Change Concerns
item.description: For the first time in over four decades, Panama’s crucial ocean upwelling event did not occur in 2025, a surprising disruption that scientists have linked to weakening wind patterns. The seasonal upwelling, which has reliably fueled the Gulf of Panama’s ecosystem by bringing cold, nutrient-rich waters to the surface, is critical for local fisheries and coral reefs. Researchers at the Smithsonian Tropical Research Institute (STRI) noted that the usual cooling effect was missing, reducing ocean productivity and potentially threatening marine life. The drop in wind strength, a possible result of climate change, is believed to be the cause of this unexpected event, which disrupted the region’s ecological balance and could have long-lasting effects on coastal communities that depend on fishing. This discovery underscores the vulnerability of tropical upwelling systems and calls for more effective monitoring and climate observation in these vital ocean regions. The research highlights how sw

item.title: Suspect in D.C. Correspondents’ Dinner shooting traveled from California and allegedly planned attack in advance
item.description:
Authorities say Cole Tomas Allen, a 31-year-old Caltech graduate and tutor from Torrance, California, traveled across the country by train before carrying out a shooting near the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner in Washington, D.C. Investigators believe Allen brought two firearms with him and managed to bypass multiple layers of event security before being stopped by federal agents just outside the ballroom where President Donald Trump and other high-ranking officials were উপস্থিত. One officer was shot at close range but survived due to a ballistic vest.
According to officials, Allen had written a manifesto prior to the attack, which he reportedly shared with family members. A relative alerted police in Connecticut after recognizing the seriousness of the document while following news coverage of the incident. While the full contents of the manifesto have not been publicly released, reports indicate that Allen expressed intent to target members of the Trump administratio

item.title: Threats against President Trump highlight growing political violence in the U.S.
item.description: Donald Trump has faced multiple threats in recent years, with the latest occurring on April 25, 2026, during the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner. This marks the third such incident in three years. The shooting incident highlights the increasing levels of political violence in the United States, a trend that has escalated since the January 6, 2021, Capitol insurrection. Experts, including James Piazza, a political violence scholar at Penn State, point to factors like political polarization, divisive rhetoric, the spread of disinformation, and attacks on democratic norms as key drivers of this violence. Researchers note that political violence is increasingly normalized in the U.S., with both sides of the political spectrum viewing each other as immoral. The rise of social media has also fueled the spread of extremist views, further motivating violent acts. While political violence in the U.S. is not unprecedented, its current form and scale, particularly the role of socia

item.title: Profile and Details of the Suspected Gunman
item.description: The news article discusses information surrounding a suspected gunman involved in a recent incident. While details remain limited, authorities have described the suspect as acting alone, with no confirmed affiliations to any organized groups. Reports suggest the individual may have been motivated by personal grievances or mental health issues. Law enforcement has been investigating the suspect's background, movements, and possible triggers leading to the event. Officials have also urged the public to remain cautious but not alarmed, emphasizing that the situation is under control. Community responses vary, with local residents expressing concern about safety and support for increased security measures. The article aimed to provide a clear timeline of events and insights from law enforcement sources to inform the public of ongoing investigations and precautionary steps.

item.title: Dodgers Respond to Cubs' Manager's Criticism of Shohei Ohtani's Rule
item.description: The Los Angeles Dodgers have responded to criticism from Chicago Cubs manager Craig Counsell regarding the 'Ohtani Rule,' a set of regulations aimed at allowing two-way players like Shohei Ohtani to stay in games longer. The rule, which permits a two-way player to continue as a designated hitter after leaving the mound, has been in effect since 2019. Despite its long-standing approval and input from various teams, Counsell voiced his dissatisfaction with the rule in April 2026. Dodgers' President of Baseball Operations, Andrew Friedman, expressed confusion over the timing of the criticism, emphasizing that the rule was carefully considered and beneficial for the sport. The rule was designed to maximize Ohtani's unique value as both a pitcher and a hitter. Friedman also clarified misconceptions regarding the Dodgers' roster structure, noting that the team doesn't carry an additional reliever due to Ohtani. Instead, the rule allows Ohtani to play both positions, providing a competitive a

item.title: OMODA & JAECOO Launch International Hybrid Vehicle Test Drive Across China
item.description:
OMODA & JAECOO, a brand under China’s Chery Group, is launching a large-scale international test drive event called the “9 Countries Super Hybrid Marathon,” bringing together media representatives from nine countries including Spain, France, Brazil, and Australia. The event, held in China, is designed to showcase the capabilities of the company’s hybrid electric vehicle (HEV) lineup, specifically the OMODA 5, JAECOO 5, and JAECOO 7 models powered by its Super Hybrid System (SHS).
The test drive spans approximately 625 kilometers and includes a mix of highways, mountain roads, and lakeside routes to simulate real-world driving conditions. Participants will evaluate key performance aspects such as power delivery, ride comfort, and noise levels. The route begins in Wuhu and includes scenic areas like Chaohu Lake and Huoshan, combining technical testing with a visually immersive journey documented by a professional film crew.
The event emphasizes three main elements: rigorous real-world

item.title: Human Origins Likely Shaped by Multiple Interconnected Populations in Africa
item.description: Recent genetic research from the University of California, Davis challenges the long-standing view that all modern humans descended from a single ancestral population in Africa. By analyzing genomes from diverse African populations, including 44 newly sequenced Nama individuals from southern Africa, researchers discovered that early Homo sapiens evolved from multiple intermingling groups rather than one isolated population. These populations exchanged genes over hundreds of thousands of years, even after beginning to diverge around 120,000 to 135,000 years ago. The findings suggest that human evolution resembles a network of interconnected branches rather than a simple family tree. This model helps explain the genetic diversity observed in modern humans without invoking contributions from unknown archaic hominins. The study also has implications for interpreting the fossil record, indicating that early human populations with varying physical traits may have shared a common genetic back

item.title: Informant Contacts Connecticut Authorities Following Shooting Near White House Correspondents’ Dinner
item.description: A person who had information about a shooting incident near the White House Correspondents’ Dinner contacted local police in Connecticut, opening a new lead in the investigation. The shooting occurred outside the Washington Hilton, where President Donald Trump was attending the high‑profile dinner, and prompted a rapid law‑enforcement response that led to the suspect’s arrest. The gunman, identified as 31‑year‑old Cole Allen of Torrance, California, allegedly opened fire in the hotel lobby before being subdued by security forces. A Secret Service officer was struck by a bullet but survived thanks to a bulletproof vest.Authorities have described Allen as a lone actor who appeared to be targeting members of the Trump administration, and investigators have found anti‑Trump and anti‑Christian content in his online writings. The incident unfolded Saturday night, shortly before Trump was to speak, and led to the evacuation of top government officials and the rescheduling of the annual dinner

item.title: Pittsburgh Steelers 2026 Draft: Day Three Selections and Options
item.description: The Pittsburgh Steelers are heading into Day Three of the 2026 NFL Draft with five remaining picks. While the team has already filled many needs, it has several options to complete its rookie class. The focus could be on depth in key positions, such as running back, wide receiver, tight end, defensive line, inside linebacker, and safety. Key prospects for the Steelers include versatile running back Eli Heidenreich from Navy, and wide receivers Kaden Wetjen from Iowa and Barion Brown from LSU, both known for their return capabilities. At tight end, Khalil Dinkins of Penn State could be a late-round target. On defense, prospects like defensive linemen Rayshaun Benny of Michigan and James Thompson Jr. of Illinois, and linebacker Jaden Dugger from Louisiana offer potential as future contributors. Pittsburgh may also consider punters like Brett Thorson from Georgia. As the draft nears its conclusion, the Steelers will likely prioritize special teams and depth, with a few picks potentially g

item.title: Protester Arrested After Spraying Chemical Agent on LAPD Officers during Demonstration
item.description: A protester was arrested after spraying a chemical agent on two Los Angeles Police Department officers outside the Metropolitan Detention Center on April 25, 2026. The confrontation took place during a demonstration against federal immigration policies. The LAPD officers had been clearing debris from the roadway when they were surrounded by protesters. As part of an effort to de-escalate, the officers began to drive away when the protester approached their vehicle and sprayed the chemical, nearly causing a traffic accident. The officers were treated for exposure to the chemical agent and were released after being evaluated. The protester was taken into custody on suspicion of assault with a deadly weapon. No further details about the arrest or the identity of the individual have been released. The incident adds to the ongoing tensions around protests related to federal immigration policies and the Trump administration's mass deportation efforts.

item.title: Shots Fired Near White House Correspondents' Dinner; Trump Evacuated Safely
item.description: The White House Correspondents' Association dinner at the Washington Hilton turned chaotic on April 25, 2026, when a gunman armed with a shotgun, handgun, and knives attempted to breach a security checkpoint outside the ballroom. President Donald Trump, attending his first such event as president, was seated on stage moments before loud bangs echoed through the venue around 8:36 p.m. Secret Service agents swiftly rushed Trump, Vice President JD Vance, First Lady Melania Trump, and several cabinet members off the stage amid shouts of 'Get down!' and 'Stay down!' Guests in formal attire dove under tables as plates crashed to the floor and big band music halted abruptly. The suspect, identified as 31-year-old Cole Allen acting as a lone gunman, fired shots that injured one law enforcement officer before being subdued and taken into custody. No attendees were harmed, thanks to rapid security response at the checkpoint. Trump was whisked away in his motorcade to the White House, where he la

item.title: Trump's Plan to Bail Out Spirit Airlines Sparks Division Within His Party
item.description: President Donald Trump's suggestion this week to 'just buy' bankrupt Spirit Airlines has led to significant debate within the Republican Party. While Trump argued that a bailout would protect jobs and preserve the airline’s operations, his proposal has sparked backlash from members of his own party. Critics claim that the move would go against the GOP's long-standing stance against government intervention in private business. The administration's internal division on the issue reflects the broader ideological rift within the Republican Party, with some seeing it as an opportunity to safeguard jobs in a key industry, while others worry about the precedent it would set for future government bailouts. The situation has raised questions about the role of government in the private sector and whether such interventions are acceptable under conservative principles.

item.title: US Government Expands AI-Based Surveillance Programs, Raising Privacy Concerns
item.description: Privacy and technology law expert Anne Toomey McKenna has raised concerns about the U.S. government's growing investment in AI-driven surveillance technologies. She points out that the government is increasingly able to purchase sensitive data from commercial sources without the same legal restrictions that apply to data collected directly. In addition, federal agencies, particularly the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) and its branch Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), are expanding direct data collection capabilities through partnerships with private tech companies. These investments are fueled by substantial congressional funding, including a record $165 billion annual budget for DHS in 2025. Leaked documents indicate these programs include AI-enhanced airport surveillance, phone-based biometric scanning, and AI platforms that analyze 911 call data to predict incident trends, a form of predictive policing. McKenna warns that this use of commercial data bypasses constitut

item.title: Suspect in White House Correspondents’ Dinner Shooting Held as Lone Actor, Investigation Under Way
item.description: A suspect in a shooting incident at the White House Correspondents’ Dinner in Washington, D.C., appears to have acted alone, according to local police officials cited in the Wall Street Journal’s live coverage. The individual, later identified as Cole Allen, a 31‑year‑old man from Torrance, California, was detained after allegedly confronting a Secret Service checkpoint near the Washington Hilton, where the annual dinner was taking place. Authorities report that he was armed with a shotgun, a handgun, and several knives and rushed directly toward security, prompting a rapid response that led to his apprehension and the evacuation of President Donald Trump and other attendees. The president was not injured, though at least one law enforcement officer was struck in the chest by a round but survived thanks to a protective vest. Investigators say the suspect is not cooperating and do not yet have a clear motive; early assessments suggest the attack may have been a lone‑wolf operation rathe

item.title: Global Nuclear News and Events Roundup
item.description: This comprehensive roundup details recent developments and ongoing events related to nuclear energy, safety, and global geopolitical implications. Key highlights include upcoming discussions on the Fukushima disaster anniversary, a webinar focusing on nuclear safety protocols, and an analysis of the evolving landscape of nuclear power in relation to climate change mitigation efforts. The site provides a massive archive of articles and resources, covering everything from Fukushima's ongoing challenges and responses to discussions around the development and deployment of Small Modular Reactors (SMRs). The site also highlights the increasing relevance of nuclear power in achieving global decarbonization goals, while acknowledging the ongoing debates surrounding safety, waste disposal, and proliferation risks. It also addresses political events impacting nuclear issues, like the upcoming US elections and their potential effects on nuclear policy. The site serves as a valuable resource for

item.title: Staffing Shortages and Policy Changes Impact National Parks and California Communities
item.description: Recent changes in national park staffing and policy, coupled with a government shutdown, have led to a concerning situation impacting California's iconic landscapes and communities. While the parks appear outwardly ‘normal’ to visitors, significant challenges lie beneath the surface. The government shutdown, combined with hiring freezes and policy shifts, has resulted in reduced staff levels across numerous national parks. This scarcity of personnel has led to curtailed services, deferred maintenance, and increased risks for visitors. Park rangers, essential for visitor safety, resource protection, and infrastructure upkeep, are stretched thin. The shift in policy regarding the types of contracts and staffing models has exacerbated the issue, creating uncertainty among existing employees and hindering recruitment efforts. Residents in communities surrounding national parks are also feeling the effects, as tourism-dependent businesses struggle with reduced visitor numbers and the potent

item.title: Nine Injured in Shooting Near Indiana University, No Suspects Arrested
item.description: Early Sunday morning, nine individuals were injured in a mass shooting near Indiana University in Bloomington, Indiana. The incident occurred shortly after midnight during a celebration following the 'Little 500' college cycling race. Police responded to reports of gunfire along Kirkwood Avenue, discovering multiple victims, six of whom were transported to local hospitals by ambulance. Witnesses reported the altercation began between two women at the event, escalating when one woman drew a firearm and started shooting. The extent of the victims' injuries has not been fully disclosed, and authorities have not yet made any arrests. This shooting adds to recent incidents of violent crimes in the U.S., highlighting ongoing concerns about public safety at large gatherings. The Bloomington Police Department continues to investigate the circumstances surrounding the shooting, and the community remains on alert as law enforcement searches for the suspect. Authorities urge anyone with informati

item.title: Extreme Weather Events Linked to Increased Risk of Heart Disease
item.description: A recent study highlights the growing connection between extreme weather events, such as heatwaves, severe cold, and heavy rainfall, and an increased risk of heart disease, particularly in middle-aged and older adults. Conducted across 157 cities in China, the research shows that extreme temperatures and precipitation directly impact heart health. The study reveals that heatwaves above 100.4°F are the most dangerous, causing a significant increase in heart disease cases. Extreme cold, below 14°F, and heavy rain events also contribute to a rise in heart-related issues, though to a lesser extent. Vulnerable populations, including those nearing retirement, smokers, and those living in areas with higher pollution levels, are most at risk. The study's findings stress the need for targeted interventions to protect high-risk individuals during extreme weather conditions. Measures like weather alerts, better city planning, and improved infrastructure could mitigate the impact of these weather

item.title: Overview of Threats Against Trump Since White House Correspondents' Dinner Shooting
item.description: Over the years following the shooting incident at the White House Correspondents' Dinner, former President Donald Trump has been the target of multiple threats to his safety. These threats have varied in severity and have prompted law enforcement agencies to monitor potential risks closely. Security measures around Trump, both during his presidency and afterwards, have been heightened in response to credible intelligence suggesting ongoing concerns about his personal safety. The article highlights several instances in which threats were intercepted or addressed before they could pose immediate danger, emphasizing the persistent nature of threats against high-profile political figures. It also explores the psychological and political impacts these threats have on public life and political discourse, as well as the measures taken to prevent harm. The reporting draws attention to the broader implications of political violence and the challenges of balancing security with accessibility in

item.title: Two San Diego Men Sentenced for Smuggling Endangered Birds Across the U.S. Border
item.description: Two men from San Diego were sentenced for smuggling endangered exotic birds into the United States, reflecting an ongoing wildlife trafficking problem at the U.S.-Mexico border. Ricardo Alonzo received a three-month prison sentence for illegally bringing 17 birds—including red-lored Amazon parrots, yellow-crowned Amazon parrots, and Burrowing parakeets—without proper quarantine. The birds were found hidden in bags under his car seat, along with three chickens used as decoys. Tragically, the two red-lored Amazon parrots died during the transport. The surviving birds were sent to the Bronx Zoo. In a separate case, Carlos Abundez was fined $74,330 for smuggling 14 sedated keel-billed toucans in the dashboard of his car, some of which suffered injuries such as broken legs and tails. The illegal trade threatens protected species and increases the risk of zoonotic diseases entering the U.S., while driven by demand for exotic pets. Authorities noted that these incidents are part of a larger p

item.title: Sebastian Sawe Sets First Official Sub-Two-Hour Marathon Record at London Marathon
item.description:
Kenyan runner Sebastian Sawe made history at the London Marathon by becoming the first athlete to complete a marathon in under two hours during an official competitive race. Sawe finished with a time of 1:59:30, marking a groundbreaking achievement in long-distance running and setting a new world record. His performance surpasses the previous record of 2:00:35 set by the late Kelvin Kiptum at the Chicago Marathon.
Breaking the two-hour barrier in a sanctioned race has long been considered one of the ultimate milestones in athletics. While previous attempts—such as Eliud Kipchoge’s 1:59:40 in 2019—demonstrated that it was physically possible, those efforts were not recognized as official world records due to controlled conditions, including pacing assistance and course design. Sawe’s accomplishment stands apart because it was achieved under standard competitive rules, making it eligible for official recognition.
The race in London provided ideal conditions, and Sawe maintained a consi

item.title: Discovery of Blood Vessels in T. rex Bones Provides New Insight into Dinosaur Biology
item.description: Scientists have made a groundbreaking discovery in the study of Tyrannosaurus rex fossils, revealing preserved blood vessels within a rib of a T. rex known as 'Scotty,' the largest specimen ever found. Using synchrotron X-ray imaging, the researchers were able to analyze the dense fossil without damaging it. The preserved vessels, which appear to be part of a healing process from an ancient bone fracture, could offer unprecedented insights into how large dinosaurs recovered from injuries. This discovery challenges previous understanding of dinosaur biology and provides new avenues for studying soft tissues in fossils. The study suggests that such preserved blood vessels may also offer clues about the relationship between dinosaurs and modern animals, including birds. Additionally, the findings could help researchers better understand the fossilization process, offering a more complete picture of how ancient creatures lived and healed. This breakthrough highlights the increasing role of

item.title: Timeline of Events Surrounding the Incident at the White House Correspondents' Dinner
item.description: The White House Correspondents' Dinner, an annual event where journalists and political figures gather, became the scene of a shocking event when a shooting unfolded. The situation began to unravel as law enforcement responded quickly to reports of gunfire, leading to an immediate lockdown at the venue. The authorities worked tirelessly to secure the area and ensure the safety of those present, while providing updates to the public. Details emerged about the suspect and the motives behind the act. Although no one was severely injured in the incident, it raised significant concerns about security at high-profile events, prompting discussions about better safeguards for public gatherings. As the investigation progressed, it became clear that the shooting was not related to terrorism but was instead an isolated incident, leading to the arrest of the suspect. The dinner, which was meant to celebrate the role of the media in American society, ended on a somber note, with many attendees and

item.title: What’s Next for Apple’s Hardware Strategy Under John Ternus?
item.description: Apple’s future hardware strategy is set to shift under new leadership, as John Ternus, a longtime hardware executive, is poised to take over as CEO later this year, succeeding Tim Cook. Ternus, who joined Apple in 2001, has a deep background in hardware engineering and played a key role in products like the AirPods, Apple Watch, and Vision Pro. His appointment marks a shift in focus back to Apple’s hardware innovation, especially as the company faces mounting pressure to remain competitive in the rapidly evolving AI space. Rather than competing directly with companies building massive AI models, Ternus is expected to push Apple to focus on AI-powered devices—smart products like wearables, home assistants, and AI-enhanced AirPods. Speculation is already growing about upcoming product releases, including foldable iPhones and possibly even smart glasses or a wearable pendant with a built-in camera. Additionally, Ternus has a long-standing interest in robotics, which could lead Apple to ex

item.title: Weekly American Culture Quiz Highlights Baseball and Restaurant Updates
item.description: Fox News Digital's weekly American Culture Quiz offers readers an interactive way to test their knowledge on current American trends, history, and popular culture. This edition focuses on baseball highlights and restaurant transformations, among other national topics. The quiz encourages participants to engage with cultural knowledge while staying updated on trending stories. It is designed to reflect both historical touchpoints and contemporary events in the United States, providing a mix of entertainment and education. Kelly McGreal, a production assistant on the lifestyle team, contributes to curating these quizzes. Participants can explore a variety of topics ranging from sports milestones to food industry innovations. The quiz also links to additional quizzes and news content, giving readers the opportunity to engage regularly with American culture through Fox News Digital.

item.title: Mobile Apps to Break the Habit of Excessive Social Media Scrolling
item.description: Doomscrolling—the habit of endlessly scrolling through social media content—has become a common behavior, with 64% of Americans reporting that they engage in it. Experts warn that prolonged exposure to negative or stressful content online can impact mental health, focus, and sleep quality. To address this, several apps offer more productive ways to spend time on your phone. For creative breaks, Dudel Draw presents daily shapes that users transform into unique drawings, which can be shared for friendly competition. Radio Garden allows users to explore over 25,000 live radio stations worldwide, providing global perspectives without social media. Elevate offers cognitive training with games designed to improve focus, memory, and problem-solving skills. Vocabulary helps expand language skills with daily word lessons and mini-games, while Seterra improves geography knowledge through interactive map quizzes. NYT Games features word, logic, and number games like crosswords and Wordle, providi

item.title: Details emerging on suspect in White House Correspondents’ Dinner shooting
item.description: Authorities have provided preliminary information about the individual suspected in the shooting incident near the White House Correspondents’ Dinner in Washington, D.C. The suspect, a guest staying at the Washington Hilton, was apprehended after attempting to breach a Secret Service security checkpoint outside the ballroom where President Donald Trump and other dignitaries were gathered. Armed with a shotgun, a handgun, and several knives, the man opened fire, striking a Secret Service officer who was wearing a bulletproof vest and survived with non–life‑threatening injuries. President Trump later posted photos of the suspect on Truth Social showing him lying face down on the floor, restrained by security personnel. The suspect, reported to be from California, is currently in custody and undergoing medical assessment, facing charges related to using a firearm during a violent crime and assaulting federal officers with a deadly weapon. Investigators have not yet established a clear mot

item.title: Trump's Washington DC Beautification Efforts Earn Unexpected Praise from Democrats
item.description: President Donald Trump's efforts to revitalize Washington, D.C. ahead of the nation’s 250th anniversary have sparked unusual praise from Democrats. Several long-neglected landmarks, including the Meridian Hill Park fountain, have been restored as part of a broader initiative to beautify the city. The fountain, one of the longest in North America, had been dry for years after being closed for renovations. Its restoration, along with the revitalization of other fountains in the city, has been met with positive feedback even from critics of Trump’s administration. Democrats, like Carter Christensen from ActBlue and liberal strategist Tré Easton, expressed their approval of the restoration projects, calling them long overdue. The Trump administration has also backed other major infrastructure projects in the capital, including a proposed Triumphal Arch, White House renovations, and other construction works aimed at enhancing the city’s appearance and safety. These efforts are part of the l

item.title: Experts Criticize Australia's $4.2 Billion Investment in US Nuclear Submarines
item.description: A new contract between the US and Australia has stirred controversy as experts criticize Australia's $4.2 billion taxpayer-funded payment for nuclear-powered submarines. The deal, signed as part of the AUKUS security pact, aims to acquire Virginia-class submarines from the US by 2032. However, critics, including Professor Mark Beeson, argue that the investment may be a mistake. Beeson believes that the submarines will become obsolete before they even arrive, overtaken by cheaper drone technology. Additionally, he raised concerns about the potential security risks posed by nuclear-armed submarines in Australia. Production delays in the US shipyards, which are responsible for the construction, further complicate the issue, with the production rate falling far below targets. Australia's dependence on the US for the submarines also highlights concerns over national sovereignty and security, as political figures like Andrew Hastie point out the risks of outsourcing defense capabilities to W

item.title: Authorities Investigate Home Connected to WHCD Shooting Suspect
item.description: CNN reports from outside a residence connected to Cole Allen, the 31-year-old individual taken into custody by U.S. authorities following the shooting incident at the White House Correspondents’ Dinner (WHCD). The news coverage highlights the law enforcement investigation surrounding Allen, who is suspected of involvement in the incident. Multiple video clips provide updates on the investigation, including statements from political figures and law enforcement officials, discussions of witness interviews, and broader political context related to the event. President Trump stated that no prior warning of threats was received before the WHCD shooting incident. The ongoing inquiry includes examination of possible motives, suspect background, and the security measures in place at the event. The news coverage also touches on related political reactions and other national security discussions prompted by the incident. CNN emphasizes the seriousness of the investigation while maintaining cover

item.title: Infowars Bankruptcy Plan Allows The Onion to Acquire Brand, Alex Jones Responds with Sandy Hook Attacks
item.description: Far-right figure Alex Jones is facing the imminent loss of his Infowars media empire due to spreading false and defamatory information about the families of the victims of the 2012 Sandy Hook Elementary School shooting. Following a court-ordered payment of roughly $1 billion in damages after two separate trials, a bankruptcy plan has emerged that will allow satirical news outlet The Onion to acquire the Infowars brand on a six-month license, with profits going to the Sandy Hook families. Jones has repeatedly denied the tragic event’s reality and continues to harass the families with his disinformation. He reacted with a meltdown on his broadcast after the deal was announced, claiming it was a defamatory attempt to impersonate him. This outburst was followed by further attacks on the Sandy Hook families, echoing previous lies about 'professional shooters' and false police reports. The families have previously testified about the immense emotional distress caused by Jones's actions, emph

item.title: Russell Brand Faces Criticism Over Bible Reading and Past Sexual Misconduct Confession
item.description: Russell Brand, 50, the English actor turned right-wing podcaster, faced online ridicule after a televised interview in which he struggled to find a relevant Bible passage during his appearance on Piers Morgan's show. Brand had brought the Bible to a sexual assault and rape hearing earlier in February, and when asked which passages were meaningful to him, he spent approximately 90 seconds flipping through pages before finally selecting one. The awkward moment drew widespread commentary on social media, with many highlighting it as an example of performative Christianity. The interview follows Brand’s previous shocking confession on Megyn Kelly’s show, where he admitted to having sexual encounters he described as 'exploitative' with a 16-year-old girl when he was 30. He acknowledged the moral and legal wrongfulness of his actions. Brand, who became a born-again Christian in 2024 and has associated with some MAGA supporters, is set to face trial later this year in the U.K. Morgan also que

item.title: Armed suspect arrested after shooting incident disrupts White House Correspondents’ Dinner
item.description:
An armed suspect was taken into custody after a shooting incident disrupted the White House Correspondents’ Dinner in Washington, DC, where President Donald Trump, cabinet members, journalists, and guests were उपस्थित. According to authorities, the suspect—identified as 31-year-old Cole Tomas Allen from California—stormed a security checkpoint carrying a shotgun, a handgun, and multiple knives. He exchanged gunfire with law enforcement before being apprehended.
President Trump was quickly escorted off the stage by Secret Service agents and was unharmed. Other officials, including Vice President JD Vance and members of the cabinet, were also evacuated as the situation unfolded. A Secret Service officer was struck by gunfire but was protected by a ballistic vest and later released from the hospital.
Law enforcement officials believe Allen acted alone and had been staying at the Washington Hilton, where the event was held. Authorities secured his hotel room and launched an investigation

item.title: Rising River Temperatures Increase Feeding Rates of Invasive Northern Pike in Alaska
item.description:
Rising temperatures in Alaska’s freshwater systems are significantly altering predator-prey dynamics, particularly involving invasive northern pike and native salmon populations. A study led by the University of Alaska Fairbanks examined pike in the Deshka River and found that warmer water temperatures are increasing the fish’s metabolic rates, causing them to consume more prey. By comparing stomach content data from 2021–2022 with samples collected roughly a decade earlier, researchers observed a clear increase in feeding across all age groups. The most dramatic change occurred among younger pike, which were found to eat about 63% more fish than in the past.
This trend is linked to long-term warming patterns in the region. Average summer air temperatures in the study area have risen by about 3°F since 1919, with recent years continuing to exceed historical norms. As water temperatures rise alongside air temperatures, pike require more energy to sustain themselves, leading to more agg

item.title: Brush Fire in Carbon Canyon, Orange County Prompts Evacuations
item.description: On Saturday, April 25, 2026, a brush fire erupted in the Carbon Canyon area of unincorporated Brea, Orange County, prompting evacuation warnings for several nearby communities. Reported shortly after 3 p.m. along Carbon Canyon Road near Valencia Avenue, the fire quickly spread northeast toward Olinda Village. Evacuation alerts were issued for residents of Olinda Village, Hollydale Mobile Home Park, and the Brea Hills area, as well as for the El Rodeo Equestrian Center. By 5 p.m., warnings were reduced to only Olinda Village and Hollydale Mobile Home Park. The Red Cross established an evacuation center at the Chino Hills Community Center to assist displaced residents. Firefighters battled the blaze both on the ground and from the air, with light rain providing some assistance in controlling the fire. Authorities closed Carbon Canyon Road between Brea Hills Avenue and Ruby Street, urging drivers to seek alternate routes. As of the latest reports, the fire had consumed approximately 200 a

item.title: Study suggests EPA in fish oil may impair brain repair after repeated head injuries
item.description:
A new study from the Medical University of South Carolina challenges the widely held belief that fish oil supplements are universally beneficial for brain health. Researchers found that eicosapentaenoic acid (EPA), a major omega-3 fatty acid in fish oil, may actually hinder the brain’s ability to recover from repeated mild traumatic brain injuries (TBIs). The findings, published in Cell Reports, highlight a context-dependent effect in which EPA interferes with key repair mechanisms in the brain.
The research focused on how the brain repairs its blood vessels after injury. In experimental models, elevated levels of EPA were linked to reduced vascular stability, impaired healing signals, and increased accumulation of tau protein, which is associated with cognitive decline and conditions such as chronic traumatic encephalopathy (CTE). Mice exposed to long-term fish oil supplementation showed poorer neurological performance and reduced spatial learning after repeated head impacts.
Furthe

item.title: Linux 7.1 Removes Legacy ISDN and Obsolete Network Drivers
item.description: The Linux kernel has recently undergone significant cleanup with the removal of the old ISDN subsystem and several outdated network drivers, primarily related to PCMCIA-era network adapters. Linus Torvalds approved a pull request that removed these drivers to streamline the kernel and reduce maintenance burdens. The removal affects drivers for hardware such as the 3com 3c509/3c515/3c574/3c589 series, AMD Lance, AMD NMCLAN, SMSC SMC9194/SMC91C92, Fujitsu FMVJ18X, and the 8390 AX88190/Ultra/WD80X3 adapters. Additionally, amateur radio support, legacy ATM device drivers, and bus mouse support have been eliminated. This update also begins phasing out Intel 486 CPU support and dropping support for Russia's Baikal CPUs. The removal, driven in part by AI-generated bug reports and extensive code fuzzing, lightens the kernel by over 138,000 lines. Users of older hardware can still run previous Linux versions or compile older kernel versions if necessary, ensuring retro computing or specialized

item.title: California Man Identified as Suspect in White House Correspondents' Dinner Shooting
item.description:
Authorities have identified Cole Allen, a 31-year-old computer scientist from Torrance, California, as the suspect in a shooting incident that occurred during the White House Correspondents' Dinner in Washington, D.C. According to officials, Allen allegedly rushed a Secret Service checkpoint at the Washington Hilton Hotel while armed with multiple weapons and opened fire on a Secret Service officer. The officer was struck in a ballistic vest and transported to a hospital, while Allen was taken into custody after agents returned fire. No other injuries were reported.
Allen’s background reveals a history of academic and professional achievements. He studied mechanical engineering at the California Institute of Technology (Caltech), graduating in 2017, and later earned a master’s degree in computer science from California State University, Dominguez Hills, in 2025. His experience includes work as a tutor and involvement in game development, including creating physics-based shooter-style

item.title: Trump Cancels Iran Talks, Claims Iran Quickly Offered Better Terms
item.description: On April 25, 2026, President Donald Trump canceled in-person ceasefire talks between U.S. envoys and Iranian officials, claiming that Iran presented much better terms immediately after the talks were called off. Trump explained that after canceling the negotiations, Iran sent a new proposal just ten minutes later, which he described as significantly improved. Earlier that day, Trump had instructed officials Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner not to travel to Pakistan for the scheduled talks. Iran’s Foreign Minister, Abbas Araghchi, who was in Islamabad at the time, expressed uncertainty over whether the U.S. was genuinely committed to diplomacy. Trump justified his decision to cancel the talks, citing the time and effort required for travel, and stated on his social media platform that if Iran wanted to talk, they could simply make a phone call instead. This statement reflects the ongoing tensions and complex dynamics in the U.S.-Iran relationship, especially regarding diplomatic negotiat

item.title: SusHi Tech Tokyo 2026 Highlights Emerging AI, Robotics, and Resilience Innovations
item.description: SusHi Tech Tokyo 2026 is positioning itself as a leading technology event by focusing on four clearly defined areas: AI, robotics, urban resilience, and entertainment. The conference emphasizes practical, real-world applications rather than vague trends, featuring sessions led by industry leaders such as Howard Wright from Nvidia, Rob Chu from AWS, and Eric Benhamou from Benhamou Global Ventures. Attendees can see AI deployed at scale, explore physical AI through interactive robotics demonstrations by companies like Nissan and Isuzu, and participate in VR disaster simulations and site visits to Tokyo's flood-control infrastructure. In entertainment, AI is helping translate manga globally, generate music from text prompts, and adapt Japanese intellectual property into anime for international audiences. The conference also accommodates remote participants via interactive devices and online streaming. Additionally, the Tokyo Metropolitan Government will host a global city leaders’ summit

item.title: LA Students Receive Free Prom Dresses and Tuxedos at Annual Giveaway Event
item.description: Hundreds of high school students from across Los Angeles County attended the 10th Annual Prom Dress & Tux Gift-Away and Resource Fair at the Sheraton Gateway Los Angeles Hotel on April 25, 2026. Hosted by Positive Results Center, the event provided free formal prom attire to students, including dresses, tuxedos, shoes, and accessories. Volunteers helped students find the right outfits, offering a wide range of sizes and styles, including dresses, pantsuits, and accessories such as shoes, purses, and jewelry. Positive Results Center's CEO, Kandee Rochelle Lewis, emphasized the event's goal of ensuring that all students, regardless of financial background, feel deserving of a special prom experience. In addition to the formal attire, the event also provided a space for students to take prom pictures and celebrate a milestone in their high school journey. Sponsors included Thrive Causemetics, Brotherhood Crusade, and local government officials. The event highlighted the importance of comm

item.title: Global Nuclear Power Usage Increasing Despite Historical Accidents
item.description: Forty years after the Chernobyl disaster, nuclear power is experiencing a global resurgence. The recent geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, along with the need for low-carbon energy sources, have renewed interest in nuclear energy. Currently, over 400 nuclear reactors are operational across 31 countries, with approximately 70 more under construction. Nuclear power contributes about 10% of global electricity production, roughly a quarter of all low-carbon energy sources. Safety and efficiency improvements in reactor design have made new projects more feasible. The United States remains the largest nuclear power producer, with 94 reactors accounting for around 30% of global nuclear electricity, and plans to quadruple capacity by 2050. China operates 61 reactors and is actively building nearly 40 more, aiming to surpass the U.S. France relies heavily on nuclear power, generating nearly 70% of its electricity from 57 reactors. Even Ukraine, the site of the 1986 accident, generates ab

item.title: STACX: Free CRM App Designed Specifically for Content Creators
item.description: Content creators often juggle complex business tasks such as managing brand deals, tracking deadlines, invoicing, and payment follow-ups, frequently relying on scattered tools like spreadsheets and email threads. STACX, a CRM developed by Ghanaian tech entrepreneur Dessy Ocean, aims to simplify this process. Designed specifically for content creators, STACX consolidates all aspects of brand partnerships—from initial outreach and contracts to deliverables and payments—into one platform. The app provides a pipeline view of every brand relationship, surfaces overdue payments, displays upcoming deadlines, and offers an analytics dashboard for financial insights. Recent updates include a Noir mode alongside Light and Dark themes, home screen widgets for quick earnings visibility, iCloud backup for deal data, and full iPad support with optimized layouts for both portrait and landscape orientations. STACX is free to download on the App Store, with a premium tier available for creators managin

item.title: Authorities Investigate Residence of Suspect in White House Correspondents' Dinner Shooting
item.description: On Saturday evening, April 25, 2026, a significant security incident occurred at the White House Correspondents' Dinner held at the Washington Hilton, resulting in the evacuation of President Donald Trump and other high-ranking officials. Law enforcement authorities identified the suspect as 31-year-old Cole Tomas Allen, a resident of California, who was apprehended after a confrontation with Secret Service agents at a security checkpoint. During the encounter, the assailant was found to be armed with a shotgun, a handgun, and multiple knives. One Secret Service agent was struck by gunfire but remained uninjured due to wearing a protective bulletproof vest; President Trump later confirmed that the officer was recovering well. Following the arrest, federal agents, including the FBI, initiated an investigation into the suspect's background, leading to a law enforcement presence at the individual's residence in Torrance, California. While the investigation remains ongoing to determine the

item.title: Mississippi Middle School Students Safely Stop Bus After Driver's Asthma Attack
item.description: A group of about 40 students from Hancock Middle School in Mississippi heroically intervened when their bus driver, 46-year-old Leah Taylor, suffered a severe asthma attack and lost consciousness shortly after leaving campus on Wednesday. The bus began swerving on a highway, prompting quick action from the young passengers. Sixth-grader Jackson Casnave, 12, who was seated behind the driver, immediately grabbed the steering wheel and called for help to prevent a crash. His classmate Darrius Clark, also 12, applied the brakes, guiding the bus to the median and bringing it to a safe stop. Meanwhile, Kayleigh Clark, 13 and Darrius's sister, called 911 from the back of the bus despite the chaos of screaming classmates. Eighth-grader Destiny Cornelius, 15, noticed Taylor clutching a nebulizer and administered the medication, with 13-year-old McKenzy Finch assisting by supporting the driver's head. Finch also answered Taylor's ringing phone to alert the school district's transportation team.

item.title: Security Incident at the White House Correspondents' Dinner Leads to Trump's Evacuation
item.description: A tense security situation unfolded at the White House Correspondents’ Dinner on April 25, 2026, when gunshots were fired outside the ballroom, prompting the evacuation of President Donald Trump. The incident occurred near the Washington Hilton's magnetometers, where Trump and other prominent figures, including the First Lady and White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt, were seated. The suspect, identified as a man from California, was apprehended with multiple firearms and knives. Law enforcement officials were quickly on the scene, and one officer was injured but is expected to recover. Trump, who had been planning to deliver a speech, initially wanted to continue the event, but the U.S. Secret Service decided to cancel the dinner. The suspect is in custody and will face charges. Trump later referenced the incident as being similar to a previous assassination attempt he had experienced during his 2024 campaign. Media personnel, including CNN's Wolf Blitzer, also described the ch

item.title: Astrobotic successfully tests rotating detonation rocket engine with 4,000 pounds of thrust
item.description:
Astrobotic, a U.S.-based space company, has successfully tested a new type of rocket propulsion system known as a rotating detonation rocket engine (RDRE), marking a significant step forward in space travel technology. During a series of hot-fire tests conducted at NASA’s Marshall Space Flight Center in Alabama, two prototype engines each generated more than 4,000 pounds of thrust and accumulated a combined runtime of 470 seconds, including a single continuous burn lasting 300 seconds. The tests showed no signs of damage, suggesting strong durability and reliability at this early stage.
Unlike traditional rocket engines that rely on steady combustion and exhaust, RDREs produce thrust through continuous supersonic detonations traveling around a circular chamber. These shockwaves allow the engine to operate more efficiently, potentially reducing fuel consumption while maintaining strong performance. This efficiency, combined with a more compact design, makes RDRE technology particularly

item.title: Torrance Man Arrested After Shooting Incident at White House Correspondents' Dinner
item.description: Cole Tomas Allen, a 31-year-old teacher from Torrance, California, has been identified as the suspect in a shooting incident at the White House Correspondents' Association Dinner. The chaotic scene unfolded when Allen rushed towards the event's dining area and was met with gunfire. President Donald Trump was swiftly escorted off stage. During the altercation, a police officer was shot and taken to the hospital but is expected to recover. Authorities have charged Allen with multiple offenses, including assault on a federal officer, after finding a shotgun, handgun, and multiple knives in his possession. Investigators are still looking into his motive. Notably, Allen had made a small political donation to Kamala Harris' 2024 campaign but has no known connections to extremist groups. He was previously a teacher at C2 Education and also an indie game developer. His neighbors in Torrance, where local and federal law enforcement swarmed after his arrest, expressed shock, describing him as a

item.title: White House Correspondents’ Dinner Evacuated After Gunfire, High-Profile Attendees Safe
item.description: During the White House Correspondents’ Dinner held at the Washington Hilton Hotel, gunfire erupted, prompting immediate evacuation of attendees including President Donald Trump, First Lady Melania Trump, Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr., House Majority Leader Steve Scalise, and TPUSA CEO Erika Kirk. The incident caused panic as guests took cover under tables. The suspect, identified as 31-year-old Cole Allen from California, was apprehended by law enforcement. President Trump emphasized that those who make a significant impact are often targeted by shooters and praised the Secret Service for their swift response. Several attendees had previous experiences with political violence, including assassination attempts on Trump in 2024, the assassinations of JFK and Robert F. Kennedy, Scalise’s shooting in 2017, and the recent loss of Erika Kirk’s husband. Law enforcement’s quick action prevented further harm, and all high-profile guests were safely escorted out. The

item.title: Open‑Source Project Runs Linux Kernel 6.19 Side‑by‑Side with Windows 9x on Old Hardware
item.description: An open‑source developer has created WSL9x, a project that allows the Linux kernel 6.19 to run alongside the Windows 95, 98, and Me kernels on the same machine at the same time. Rather than using full virtualization, WSL9x relies on a virtual device driver that initializes the environment, loads the Linux kernel from disk, and manages page faults and system calls. Because Windows 9x lacks the proper interrupt table support for standard Linux system calls, the project reroutes these through the fault handler instead. A small 16‑bit DOS program called wsl.com then pipes Linux terminal output back to the MS‑DOS command prompt window from which it is launched. A notable technical achievement is that WSL9x requires no hardware‑assisted virtualization and can run on processors as old as the i486, letting retro‑computing enthusiasts experiment with Linux on decade‑old or museum‑grade hardware. The code is released under the GPL‑3 license and the developer explicitly states it was written with

item.title: Microsoft adds flexible update pause options and less disruptive installs to Windows 11
item.description:
Microsoft is introducing a set of long-requested improvements to Windows Update aimed at giving users more control and reducing interruptions. The changes are currently rolling out to users in the Windows Insider Dev and Experimental channels. The most notable update is the ability to pause Windows updates repeatedly in 35-day increments, effectively allowing users to delay updates indefinitely as long as they continue extending the pause before each period expires. If users do not renew the pause, updates will resume automatically.
In addition to extended pause controls, Microsoft is making updates less intrusive in daily use. Windows 11 will now always provide options in the power menu to shut down or restart without installing updates, addressing a common frustration among users who are forced into updates at inconvenient times. There will also be an option to skip updates during the initial setup of a new device.
The company is also refining how updates are presented and installe

item.title: X‑energy and Fervo signal a new IPO phase for climate tech
item.description: Climate technology startups typically face a tough road to public markets because they are capital intensive, have long development timelines, and often tackle pollution—a cost that is poorly priced by the market. For years, this has made them an unattractive bet for many public investors. Yet the landscape appears to be shifting. This week, nuclear‑energy startup X‑energy went public, raising $1 billion in an upsized share offering that delivered a clear windfall for backers such as Amazon, while its stock surged 25% in the first hour of trading. At the same time, enhanced‑geothermal startup Fervo filed for an IPO, with private investors valuing the company at roughly $3 billion. These moves align with what climate‑tech investors predicted at the end of 2025: that public markets would start welcoming energy‑focused startups, especially those in nuclear fission and advanced geothermal. The AI‑driven boom in data‑center demand has made electricity‑intensive technologies more desirable a

item.title: Trump says no prior intelligence indicated threats before White House Correspondents’ Dinner shooting
item.description:
President Donald Trump stated that neither he nor his team had received any prior warning about potential threats before a shooting incident occurred at the White House Correspondents’ Dinner (WHCD). Speaking to CNN’s Kaitlan Collins after the event, Trump emphasized that there had been no intelligence or alerts suggesting a security risk leading up to the incident. The WHCD, a high-profile annual gathering attended by journalists, politicians, and public figures, is typically subject to extensive security measures, making the lack of prior warning notable.
The president’s remarks come in the immediate aftermath of reports that shots were fired during or around the event, raising concerns about security protocols and preparedness at one of Washington’s most prominent gatherings. While details about the shooting itself remain limited in the provided coverage, the focus of Trump’s response was on the absence of forewarning rather than the specifics of the incident or any suspects involv

item.title: Exploring Northern Cyprus: Life in a Partially Recognized State
item.description: This article chronicles a traveler's journey through Northern Cyprus, a territory recognized only by Turkey, highlighting its unique geopolitical and cultural context. The author describes crossing the border from Nicosia and the surprisingly friendly interactions with customs officers, contrasting with typical international border experiences. Northern Nicosia, the last divided capital in the world, is explored, including its bazars, the converted Santa Sophia Mosque, and modern urban developments fueled by foreign investment. The journey continues to the remote Cape Apostolos Andreas and the Karpaz National Park, home to the famous Cypriot donkeys. The author also notes the complex identity and citizenship situation of Turkish Cypriots, with most holding EU-recognized Cypriot citizenship despite living in an unrecognized state. Visits to Kyrenia and Famagusta reveal both historic ruins and the modern tourism economy. The article concludes by reflecting on the resilience and hospitali

item.title: Exploring Iran's Nuclear Program: Military Ambitions or Civilian Energy Focus?
item.description: Dr. Mehran Mostafavi, a nuclear expert and professor at Université Paris-Saclay, offers insights into the long-standing debate about Iran's nuclear program. He clarifies that while Iran's nuclear ambitions were initially tied to deterrence, especially against Israel, its military nuclear program was halted in the early 1990s. The civilian nuclear projects, such as the Bushehr power plant, remained a focus despite challenges such as the 1979 Revolution and the Iran-Iraq war. While Israeli leaders, notably Netanyahu, have repeatedly warned of an imminent Iranian nuclear bomb, Mostafavi argues that these claims are exaggerated and unfounded, citing reports from Western intelligence agencies, which have consistently reported that Iran did not have an active nuclear weapons program since 1992. He discusses the history of Iran's nuclear facilities, including Natanz and Bushehr, and addresses concerns about their proximity to populated areas. Mostafavi also highlights the international dynami

item.title: Anthropic tests AI-to-AI marketplace where agents negotiate real purchases
item.description:
Anthropic recently conducted an internal experiment called Project Deal to explore how AI agents perform in a marketplace setting where they represent human users. In this pilot, 69 Anthropic employees were each given a $100 budget in gift cards and tasked with buying and selling items through AI agents acting on their behalf. The experiment resulted in 186 completed transactions with a total value exceeding $4,000, demonstrating that AI agents can successfully negotiate and execute real economic exchanges.
The company tested four variations of the marketplace using different AI models. In the primary “real” version, transactions were honored after completion, while the other three setups were used for comparative analysis. One key finding was that more advanced AI models consistently achieved better outcomes for their users, such as negotiating more favorable prices or securing better deals. However, participants themselves generally did not notice these differences in agent performa

item.title: How AI is Impacting Human Intelligence and What We Can Do About It
item.description: Vivienne Ming, a theoretical neuroscientist and cognitive scientist, highlights the growing concern in the AI industry about whether AI is enhancing or undermining human intelligence. In an experiment comparing the predictive accuracy of AI, humans, and AI-human hybrid teams, the study found that humans, relying on instinct or limited information, performed poorly. AI models like ChatGPT and Gemini showed better results but still fell short of prediction markets. However, when human teams used AI as a sparring partner—questioning its answers, demanding evidence, and providing counterarguments—they reached insightful conclusions that neither AI nor humans could produce alone. This approach, according to Ming, promotes critical thinking and curiosity, which are often undermined by AI's instant answers. She calls this phenomenon the 'Information-Exploration Paradox,' which suggests that as access to information becomes too easy, human exploration and critical thinking are stifled. Ming ur

item.title: Trump Releases Video of Shooting Attempt at White House Correspondents' Dinner
item.description: On April 25, 2026, President Donald Trump shared a video and photo on Truth Social showing a suspect attempting to breach a security checkpoint at the White House Correspondents' Dinner held at the Washington Hilton. The footage captured a man running past law enforcement officers stationed near magnetometers, and subsequent images showed him shirtless and restrained. Trump reported that the suspect, a man in his 30s from California, had been apprehended and that authorities had also visited his apartment. During the incident, one law enforcement officer was shot but remained unharmed due to wearing a bulletproof vest. Trump emphasized the officer's safety and condition, stating he was in excellent shape. The president also highlighted plans for a new White House ballroom designed to be drone-proof and equipped with bulletproof glass, citing security concerns. When questioned about handling multiple assassination attempts, Trump said he had not become a 'basket case.' The president's s

item.title: The Evolution of Feminist Influence: From Branding to Real-World Impact
item.description: The article examines the decline of commodity feminism, a trend where feminist ideals are marketed as consumable brands rather than enacted as structural change. It highlights how figures like Sheryl Sandberg and Emma Grede built influential personal brands that promote empowerment, but increasingly face criticism for being disconnected from real-world outcomes. Sandberg's Lean In philosophy encouraged women to adapt to existing workplace systems, yet persistent structural barriers for mothers and women of color reveal limits to individual strategies. Meanwhile, Grede's comments on remote work sparked immediate backlash from Gen Z audiences, demonstrating a shift in how credibility is evaluated: authority alone no longer suffices; real-time audience response now plays a key role. The article argues that future feminist leadership must combine personal branding with tangible action, including paid parental leave, childcare support, transparent promotion practices, and advocacy for syste

item.title: Suspected Shooter Apprehended After Incident at White House Correspondents' Dinner
item.description: On April 25, 2026, Cole Tomas Allen, a 31-year-old engineer from Torrance, California, was identified as the suspected shooter at the White House Correspondents' Dinner held at the Washington Hilton. The event, attended by President Donald Trump, Vice President JD Vance, journalists, and government officials, was interrupted after shots were fired outside the ballroom. Secret Service agents immediately escorted Trump and Vance off the stage. Allen, who was armed with a shotgun, handgun, and multiple knives, charged a security checkpoint and was intercepted by the agents. One Secret Service agent sustained a minor injury but was protected by a bulletproof vest, and no other injuries were reported. Allen was taken to a local hospital for evaluation and is considered a lone actor. Allen’s background includes a mechanical engineering degree from Caltech (2017) and a master's in computer science from California State University Dominguez Hills (2025). He has worked part-time as an instructo

item.title: Los trabajadores de Amazon luchan contra la colaboración de la empresa con ICE
item.description: Los trabajadores de Amazon, incluidos conductores de reparto, empleados de almacén e ingenieros de software, se están organizando contra la relación de la empresa con ICE, la agencia de inmigración de Estados Unidos. Matt Multari, un conductor de Amazon, habló en una manifestación del Día Internacional de los Trabajadores en Nueva York, denunciando cómo la compañía utiliza datos de sus trabajadores para alimentar algoritmos que los hacen cada vez más reemplazables. Amazon Web Services, la plataforma de computación en la nube de Amazon, es contratada por ICE, y se sabe que Amazon ha trabajado con empresas como Palantir, que desarrolla tecnología de vigilancia utilizada por ICE. Los trabajadores, que en su mayoría son inmigrantes, exigen respeto y mejores condiciones laborales, enfrentándose a la automatización y la falta de seguridad laboral. Aunque han logrado algunas concesiones, como el pago durante tormentas invernales extremas, siguen luchando por un futuro más justo.

item.title: El impacto de la vigilancia digital en la seguridad y libertad del periodismo
item.description: Un informe reciente de la Federación Internacional de Periodistas alerta sobre cómo la vigilancia digital se ha convertido en una amenaza estructural para el periodismo independiente. Basado en investigaciones de 2021 a 2025, el estudio documenta que las tecnologías de espionaje, antes restringidas a agencias de inteligencia, se han comercializado y extendido a gobiernos y actores privados, afectando la seguridad de los periodistas en todo el mundo. Herramientas como Pegasus, Predator o Graphite permiten acceder a teléfonos móviles con técnicas de 'cero clic' o 'un clic', mientras que métodos tradicionales como el phishing o páginas web falsas completan un panorama de riesgos constante. La integración de inteligencia artificial en estos sistemas agrava la situación, facilitando la correlación masiva de datos, geolocalización y seguimiento automatizado, incluso en zonas de conflicto como Gaza o Ucrania, poniendo en peligro la integridad física de los profesionales. Casos en distintos pa

item.title: España aumenta la recaudación por IRPF y nuevos impuestos sin reforma fiscal integral
item.description: La no actualización de la tarifa del IRPF a la inflación en 2025 ha generado a España ingresos adicionales de casi 2.300 millones de euros, según refleja el Informe de Progreso Anual del Ministerio de Economía enviado a la Comisión Europea. Esta medida, conocida como 'no deflactación', ha incrementado la recaudación tanto del Estado como de las comunidades autónomas, sumando un aumento del 0,14% del PIB. Además, el Gobierno ha introducido modificaciones fiscales puntuales que aportan 6.200 millones anuales, incluyendo nuevas figuras tributarias y subidas de impuestos existentes. Entre ellas destacan el Impuesto Temporal de Solidaridad de las Grandes Fortunas, gravando patrimonios superiores a tres millones de euros, y el aumento de tipos en el ahorro de grandes rentas. También se han implementado cambios en el Impuesto sobre Sociedades, como la tributación mínima del 15%, y la limitación de la consolidación de pérdidas en grupo, incrementando la recaudación en miles de millones de euro

item.title: Georgina Orellano denuncia la explotación de trabajadoras sexuales bajo la mirada punitiva del feminismo institucional
item.description: En una reciente entrevista, Georgina Orellano, activista y Secretaria General Nacional del Sindicato de Trabajadores Sexuales de Argentina, habló sobre la violencia institucional y el estigma que enfrentan las trabajadoras sexuales. Asegura que, bajo el gobierno ultraderechista de Javier Milei, las compañeras están más expuestas a abusos y arbitrariedades. Orellano también critica el feminismo institucional y punitivo, que, según ella, niega la autonomía de las trabajadoras sexuales y las trata como víctimas en lugar de como trabajadoras precarizadas. Denuncia la lógica puritana que rechaza el trabajo sexual y aboga por un modelo legal que despenalice este trabajo y lo reconozca como una forma legítima de trabajo. Además, cuestiona la idea de que las mujeres no eligen ser trabajadoras sexuales, argumentando que muchas lo hacen porque es una opción más remunerada en un sistema desigual. Orellano también habla sobre la importancia de la solidaridad entre las trabajadoras sexuales y la ne

item.title: El Desierto de Atacama: El lugar más seco del mundo y su fascinante ecosistema
item.description: El Desierto de Atacama, ubicado en el norte de Chile, es reconocido como el desierto más seco del mundo fuera de los polos. Con más de 1.000 kilómetros de extensión, algunas zonas no han recibido lluvias significativas durante más de 400 años. Este fenómeno se debe a una combinación de factores geográficos y atmosféricos como la Corriente de Humboldt, la sombra orográfica de los Andes y el Anticiclón del Pacífico Sur. A pesar de su aridez extrema, el desierto alberga una sorprendente vida adaptada a estas duras condiciones. Microorganismos extremófilos sobreviven en ambientes donde la humedad es casi inexistente, y en años excepcionales, el desierto florece con una explosión de colores gracias a las lluvias de El Niño. Además, su cielo limpio y su falta de contaminación lumínica hacen del desierto un lugar ideal para la astronomía. El Desierto de Atacama también es importante para la ciencia, ya que sus condiciones similares a las de Marte han sido utilizadas para pruebas de astrobiolo

item.title: Suecia alerta sobre riesgo de escasez de combustible para vuelos debido a conflicto en Oriente Medio
item.description: El Gobierno sueco ha emitido una alerta temprana ante el riesgo de escasez de combustible para la aviación debido al conflicto bélico en Oriente Medio y al bloqueo del estrecho de Ormuz. La ministra de Energía, Ebba Busch, informó que se trata de una advertencia preventiva para que los viajeros se mantengan informados, especialmente quienes vuelen fuera de Europa. El primer ministro Ulf Kristersson aseguró que, aunque la crisis energética es global, Suecia y el resto de Europa se ven menos afectados, destacando que Suecia tiene una capacidad amplia de refinamiento y utiliza principalmente petróleo del Mar del Norte. La directora de la Agencia de Energía sueca, Caroline Asserup, señaló que no hay riesgo de racionamiento de combustible a corto plazo y que el suministro de gasolina y gasóleo en el país no está amenazado. La alerta busca anticiparse a posibles problemas de suministro internacional, pero recalcan que, por el momento, el acceso a combustibles en Suecia permanece seguro. Esta

item.title: Irán propone un plan de 14 puntos para finalizar el conflicto, mediado por Pakistán
